data.title: Write failing integration tests for Jira bridge FILE_IMPACT surfacing
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Write RED integration tests for the Jira outbound bridge FILE_IMPACT path — PUT to /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/dso.file_impact, formatted comment with UUID marker dedup, and BRIDGE_ALERT emission on PUT failure. Tests must be confirmed failing before implementation stories begin.

**Why**: This epic integrates with an external API (Jira REST). The Jira-related implementation stories (C and D) must not be written until failing integration tests exist.

**Scope**:
- IN: Integration tests exercising the bridge-outbound path for FILE_IMPACT events end-to-end against a mocked Jira; assertions on PUT endpoint URL, credential header injection, UUID marker dedup, BRIDGE_ALERT reason token
- OUT: Implementation of the bridge path (stories C and D); unit tests for pure-logic components (those live in their respective implementation stories)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, integration tests exist that exercise the outbound bridge FILE_IMPACT path end-to-end and are confirmed RED (failing)
- When this story is complete, the failing run result is recorded as a story note: `.claude/scripts/dso ticket comment <this-story-id> "RED confirmed: <test output summary>"`
- When this story is complete, tests assert: (a) PUT target URL is /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/dso.file_impact, (b) credentials are injected into the Authorization header of the PUT, (c) comment is posted with a UUID marker for dedup, (d) on simulated PUT failure a BRIDGE_ALERT event is emitted with reason FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED

## Considerations

- [Testing] Tests must be confirmed failing (RED) before any Jira-related implementation story begins
- [Reliability] Mock Jira must simulate both 200 OK (success) and 4xx/5xx (failure) paths to exercise the BRIDGE_ALERT branch

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
